mnemonics: DACS PIE - Diagram types that supplement a Use Case: Dynamic (Activity, State Machine), Static (Class), and Implementation (Package, Component, Deployment).
keyPoints: Activity Diagrams model the workflow or business logic behind a use case scenario.
State Machine Diagrams depict the different states an object can be in and the transitions between those states.
Class Diagrams define the static structure of the system by showing its object classes, attributes, operations, and relationships.
commonMistakes: Confusing Activity Diagrams with Flowcharts; Activity diagrams can show parallel flows and object flows, which are specific to UML.
Using a State Machine Diagram for an entire system instead of for individual objects that have complex, state-dependent behavior.
Creating overly complex Class Diagrams that include every possible attribute and method, rather than focusing on the key structural relationships.
